objectives: Comprehensive security audit of mx20022 ISO 20022 financial message library
Check OWASP Top 10, XXE, unsafe code, dependency vulnerabilities, input validation, financial arithmetic, CLI handling, error info leakage
constraints: Financial message parsing library (ISO 20022, SWIFT MT)
important_facts: unsafe_code = forbid at workspace level - confirmed zero unsafe blocks
quick-xml 0.37.5 does not support external entity resolution - safe against XXE
SEPA validator uses f64 for amount boundary comparison (lines 186-227 in sepa.rs)
FedNow validator correctly uses integer-cent arithmetic via parse_amount_cents()
SEPA typed path also uses f64 (lines 443-484 in sepa.rs)
No input size limits on CLI file reads (std::fs::read_to_string unbounded)
Error messages include user-controlled values in validation errors
PatternRule accepts arbitrary regex patterns in new() - but only codegen-sourced
xml_scan uses string search not full XML parsing - correct for flat ISO 20022 structure
deny.toml properly configured: unknown-registry=deny, unknown-git=deny, source restrictions active
No secrets, credentials, or hardcoded keys found in codebase
risks_issues: MEDIUM: SEPA f64 amount comparison could misclassify boundary amounts (sepa.rs:186-227, 443-484)
LOW: No file size limits in CLI commands - potential OOM with very large files (main.rs, validate.rs:146, inspect.rs:56, translate.rs:125)
LOW: Validation error messages echo user input values - minor info exposure for logs
LOW: xml_scan string-based parsing could be confused by XML comments/CDATA containing tag-like patterns
INFO: PatternRule::new() compiles arbitrary regex - safe because patterns come from XSD codegen, not runtime user input

iteration.summary: Performed deep security audit of all 7 crates. Read and analyzed every source file in mx20022-parse, mx20022-validate, mx20022-cli, mx20022-translate, and key files in mx20022-model and mx20022-codegen. Confirmed workspace-level unsafe_code=forbid with zero violations. quick-xml 0.37.5 is safe against XXE. Main finding is SEPA validator f64 arithmetic for boundary amounts (FedNow correctly uses integer-cent math). CLI lacks file size limits. No critical vulnerabilities found. Codebase has strong security posture for a financial library.
outputs_produced: Security audit report with file:line references and severity ratings
next_actions: Fix SEPA f64 amount comparisons to use integer-cent arithmetic like FedNow
Add file size limits to CLI commands
Consider sanitizing user values in error messages for production logging
